title: Change Global H5 Heading Color from Primary to Standard Text Color
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want H5 headings to use standard text colors instead of the primary brand color, so that headings are not mistaken for clickable elements and the visual hierarchy is clearer.
description:
The global `<h5>` heading style was using the primary brand color, which is conceptually reserved for interactive/clickable elements. This created visual confusion where headings looked like links or buttons. The fix changes H5 headings to use standard text colors that differ between light and dark mode.

## Color Specification

- **Light mode**: `#555555` (dark gray)
- **Dark mode**: `#CCCCCC` (light gray)

## Design Rationale

Primary color should be reserved for interactive elements (links, buttons, actionable controls). Non-interactive headings should use neutral text colors to maintain a clear visual hierarchy.
acceptance_criteria: H5 headings display in #555555 (dark gray) in light mode
H5 headings display in #CCCCCC (light gray) in dark mode
H5 headings no longer use the primary brand color
Change applies globally across all pages using H5 headings
ux_changes: Global H5 heading color changed from primary brand color to neutral text colors — #555555 for light mode and #CCCCCC for dark mode. This prevents headings from being visually confused with clickable elements.
implementation_notes: CSS global style update targeting the `h5` selector. Separate color values set for light mode and dark mode themes.
comment_summary: Developer deployed the update to the development environment and requested review in incognito mode to bypass cached styles.
